2026-03-25T21:57:38.0573806Z

2026-03-25T21:57:38.0574701Z 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0575449Z Agent: OK so the root `package.json` has `"build": "turbo build"` which means running `pnpm build` at root level will invoke turbo. When running with a filter like `pnpm build --filter=<package>...`, this gets passed to turbo through pnpm's filtering.
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0576509Z Agent: 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0577321Z Agent: But the CONTRIBUTING.md and CLAUDE.md specifically say to use `pnpm turbo build --filter=...` instead of `pnpm build --filter=...`, because the latter might not work correctly when dependencies aren't built.
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0578092Z Agent: 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0578240Z Agent: 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0579038Z Agent: The distinction is that `pnpm turbo build --filter` passes the filter directly to turbo's command, whereas `pnpm build --filter` routes it through pnpm's own filtering mechanism first, which can cause issues with dependency resolution.
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0579899Z Agent: 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0580150Z Agent: Let me check what the test documentation specifies about this.
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0580406Z 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0581013Z ✓ $ grep -n "pnpm build\|pnpm install" /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-js/azure-sdk-for-js/documentation/Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md | head -20
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0581562Z 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0582109Z ✓ $ grep -n "pnpm build\|npm run build\|pnpm turbo" /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-js/azure-sdk-for-js/documentation/steps-after-generations.md | head -20
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0582648Z 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0582771Z Agent: According to the CLAUDE.md instructions:
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0584014Z Agent: > **Building packages**: This codebase uses `pnpm` and `turbo` with workspace linking. Running `npm run clean && npm run build` under a package directory will NOT work because dependencies may not be built yet. Always use: `pnpm turbo build --filter=<package-name>... --token 1`
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0585011Z Agent: 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0585742Z Agent: However, running `pnpm build --filter=@azure-rest/purview-datamap...` from the monorepo root effectively does the same thing as `pnpm turbo build --filter=...` because the root `build` script is `turbo build`. 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0586525Z Agent: 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0587723Z Agent: Actually, I need to reconsider this. Using `pnpm build --filter` applies pnpm's workspace filtering rather than turbo's, which means it might not properly handle dependency resolution. The more reliable approach is `pnpm turbo build --filter=<package>...` since it leverages turbo's filtering directly to ensure dependencies are built in the correct order.
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0588984Z Agent: 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.0590984Z Agent: But I shouldn't get too caught up in that distinction since both approaches seem functional. The real issue I'm noticing is the inconsistency between `npm install -g @pnpm` and `npm install -g pnpm` across the documentation. I'm also spotting several other problems: outdated pnpm-lock.yaml merge conflict instructions in steps-after-generations.md, the wrong `@pnpm` reference in Generate-code-from-TypeSpec.md, and multiple docs incorrectly saying "LTS versions of Node.js" instead of "Node.js 20 or later". Incorrect `pnpm` install command**\n\nThe `code-gen-pipeline` setup block contained:\n```ps\nnpm install -g @pnpm\n```\nThe npm package `@pnpm` does not exist. The correct command is:\n```ps\nnpm install -g pnpm\n```\n\n**2. Outdated Node.js prerequisite**\n\nThe prerequisites listed \"LTS versions of Node.js\", but the repository's root `package.json` specifies `\"node\": \"\u003e=20\"`, meaning Node.js 20 or later is required. Updated to match.\n\n---\n\n### `documentation/Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md`\n\n**1. Outdated Node.js prerequisite**\n\nSame as above – \"LTS versions of Node.js\" updated to \"Node.js 20 or later\".\n\n**2. Outdated test code examples using mocha/chai instead of vitest**\n\nThe two code examples (RLC/Purview and HLC/Monitor) still used mocha and chai patterns that were replaced when the project migrated to vitest. Specific issues:\n\n- `import { assert } from \"chai\"` → `import { describe, it, assert, beforeEach, afterEach } from \"vitest\"`\n- `import { Context } from \"mocha\"` → removed; `createRecorder` now accepts `VitestTestContext` from `@azure-tools/test-recorder`\n- `new Recorder(context.currentTest)` → `new Recorder(context)` (vitest context is passed directly)\n- `beforeEach(async function (this: Context) { recorder = new Recorder(this.currentTest); }` → `beforeEach(async (ctx) =\u003e { recorder = new Recorder(ctx); }`\n- `afterEach(async function () {` → `afterEach(async () =\u003e {`\n- Import paths updated to use `.js` extensions, consistent with the ESM module convention used throughout the repository.\n- Removed the auto-generated code header comment from the monitor example (it was specific to generated code and misleading in a documentation example).\n\nThe updated patterns match what is used in actual test files in the repository (e.g., `sdk/purview/purview-datamap-rest/test/public/` and `sdk/monitor/arm-monitor/test/`).\n\n---\n\n## Checklist\n\n- [x] Documentation changes only — no source code modified\n- [x] Verified fixes against actual source code and test files in the repository\n","branch":"docs/fix-documentation-inconsistencies","draft":false,"title":"fix documentation inconsistencies in Generate-code-from-TypeSpec.md and Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md"},"name":"create_pull_request"}}}

2026-03-25T21:57:38.3093240Z 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3093463Z This PR fixes documentation inconsistencies found in two files under `documentation/`.
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3093474Z 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3093661Z ---
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3093666Z 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3093794Z ### `documentation/Generate-code-from-TypeSpec.md`
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3093806Z 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3093896Z **1. Incorrect `pnpm` install command**
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3093900Z 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3094009Z The `code-gen-pipeline` setup block contained:
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3094081Z ```ps
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3094159Z npm install -g @pnpm
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3094227Z ```
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3094378Z The npm package `@pnpm` does not exist. The correct command is:
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3094446Z ```ps
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3094520Z npm install -g pnpm
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3094586Z ```
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3094589Z 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3094678Z **2. Outdated Node.js prerequisite**
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3094682Z 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3095186Z The prerequisites listed "LTS versions of Node.js", but the repository's root `package.json` specifies `"node": ">=20"`, meaning Node.js 20 or later is required. Updated to match.
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3095190Z 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3095256Z ---
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3095259Z 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3095513Z ### `documentation/Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md`
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3095517Z 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3095604Z **1. Outdated Node.js prerequisite**
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3095608Z 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3095873Z Same as above – "LTS versions of Node.js" updated to "Node.js 20 or later".
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3095877Z 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3096047Z **2. Outdated test code examples using mocha/chai instead of vitest**
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3096051Z 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3096510Z The two code examples (RLC/Purview and HLC/Monitor) still used mocha and chai patterns that were replaced when the project migrated to vitest. Specific issues:
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3096514Z 
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3096850Z - `import { assert } from "chai"` → `import { describe, it, assert, beforeEach, afterEach } from "vitest"`
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3096919Z - `imp
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3096981Z ...
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3097116Z (truncated, showing first 65536 bytes of 73978 total)
2026-03-25T21:57:38.3097411Z ##[endgroup]
